title: 2026 FIFA World Cup Winner
description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors France at 16.4% implied probability to win the 2026 FIFA World Cup, edging Spain at 15.3% amid a tight race reflecting Europe's depth and recent qualifier dominance, with England third at 11.1%. A wave of injuries in the past week—Lamine Yamal's fitness doubts for Spain, Kylian Mbappé's concerns for France, Rodrygo ruled out for Brazil, and Jack Grealish absent for England—has leveled the field for top contenders, tempering outright favoritism just 40 days from kickoff. Argentina and Brazil share 8.6% on strong CONMEBOL qualifying runs through late April, including Argentina's clinch, while UEFA Nations League semis showcased Spain's 5-4 thriller over France. Hosts USA, Mexico, and Canada trail below 2%, underscoring power imbalances despite expanded 48-team format and group stage draws.

title: LoL: Kiwoom DRX vs Nongshim Red Force (BO3) - LCK Rounds 1-2
description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Kiwoom DRX and Nongshim Red Force in the LCK Rounds 1-2, initially scheduled for May 2 at 6:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Kiwoom DRX" if Kiwoom DRX win the match against Nongshim Red Force.

This market will resolve to "Nongshim Red Force" if Nongshim Red Force win the match against Kiwoom DRX.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Nongshim RedForce enters as trader consensus favorite with a 4-5 series record and 45% map win rate in LCK 2026 Rounds 1-2, holding mid-table position amid recent losses to T1 and Gen.G that halted their momentum against lower seeds. Kiwoom DRX struggles at 2-7 overall with just a 25% map win rate and a five-series losing streak, including a 1-2 defeat to Gen.G last week, though they hold a key edge after sweeping NS 2-0 in Week 2 via strong macro play. This BO3 rematch under Fearless Draft tests DRX's matchup-specific upset potential against NS's superior consistency, with no reported roster changes or patch shifts altering the meta significantly in the past week.

title: Eurovision Winner 2026
description:
This market will resolve to the country whose candidate for Eurovision 2026 wins.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to win Eurovision 2026 based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve "Other". All ties will be broken according to EBU's official Eurovision rules.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

title: QatarEnergy announces/resumes LNG production in Qatar by April 30?
description:
QatarEnergy, a Qatari state-owned petroleum company, announced on March 2 that it would halt production of liquefied natural gas (LNG) due to military strikes on its operating facilities (see: https://www.qatarenergy.qa/en/MediaCenter/Pages/newsdetails.aspx?ItemId=3892).

This market will resolve to “Yes” if QatarEnergy resumes production of liquefied natural gas at QatarEnergy LNG production facilities in Qatar, or officially announces that such production has resumed or will resume, by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

QatarEnergy resuming the production of other halted products, including downstream LNG-related products, or resuming transportation of LNG without resuming production, will not alone count.

An official announcement that LNG production will resume at QatarEnergy LNG production facilities in Qatar must signal the end of the total LNG production halt effective immediately or on a specific date or clearly defined time window. Mere statements that production will resume at some undefined point in the future, or that production will resume once the halt has ended, will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from QatarEnergy (https://www.qatarenergy.qa/en/Pages/vHome.aspx); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: QatarEnergy's preparations to restart LNG production at Ras Laffan facilities post a March 2026 halt from Iranian missile strikes drove trader consensus to near-certainty on resumption by April 30, as Reuters reported on April 8 that two of three trains at Qatargas-1 (QELNG North 1) had restarted amid a US-Iran ceasefire enabling safe Strait of Hormuz transits. Full capacity ramp-up may take months due to damage, but barring renewed military escalation, official confirmation disputes, or procedural resolution challenges via UMA oracle, the outcome appears locked in.

title: LoL: Weibo Gaming vs Top Esports (BO3) - LPL Group Ascend
description:

This market will resolve to "Weibo Gaming" if Weibo Gaming win the match against Top Esports.

This market will resolve to "Top Esports" if Top Esports win the match against Weibo Gaming.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Top Esports clings to third in LPL Split 2 Group Ascend standings at 6-3 series (12-6 games) after consecutive losses to Anyone's Legend and Weibo Gaming, underscoring vulnerability in their Zuian-Tian-Creme-JackeyLove-fengyue lineup amid 369's ongoing rotation. Weibo Gaming, mired at 3-7 (10-15 games) in seventh, seized momentum with a 2-0 sweep over TES on April 28—their lone bright spot versus top foes like BLG and JDG—bolstered by Jiejie jungle aggression and Xiaohu mid macro. This Bo3 rematch under Fearless Draft on patch 26.7 tests TES rebound potential against WBG upset form, with playoff seeding implications for both in the double round-robin.

eventMetadata.context_description: HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. leads trader consensus at 49% implied probability for the 2028 Republican presidential nomination, buoyed by his April congressional testimony defending the Make America Healthy Again agenda, including USPSTF reforms and nutrition policy resets, which drew Republican praise amid his first-year transformation of public health norms. Vice President J.D. Vance trails at 39% as Iran conflict scrutiny dents his frontrunner status despite strong CPAC straw poll leads, while Secretary of State Marco Rubio holds 21% on perceived diplomatic gains in the same tensions. With 2026 midterms approaching, GOP base enthusiasm for administration insiders drives the closely contested field, reflecting post-Trump succession uncertainties.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom leads trader consensus for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination on Polymarket, reflecting his national profile built through aggressive media challenges to Republican policies, robust fundraising, and executive experience as California governor ahead of his term ending in 2027. Recent appearances by Kamala Harris, Josh Shapiro, and Andy Beshear at the April National Action Network convention underscored the wide-open primary field, with polls like Echelon Insights showing Harris slightly ahead at 22% to Newsom's 21%, though markets prioritize electability over surveys. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez draws progressive support via youth and base mobilization, while Jon Ossoff benefits from Georgia's battleground success. Consolidation could hinge on 2026 midterm results in swing states, DNC endorsements, and fundraising tallies differentiating viable paths to delegates.
